hero.badge: Mui Ne + Da Lat + Saigon — 5 Days
hero.title: 5 Days — Best of Southern Vietnam + Da Lat.
hero.titleAccent: Beach Dunes, Flower City Highlands & Dragon Fruit Country.
hero.intro: Three very different Souths in five days. Mui Ne on the coast — sand dunes, fishing villages, and the Fairy Stream. Da Lat in the highlands — Crazy House, Truc Lam Pagoda by cable car, Linh Phuoc Dragon Temple, and the pine-scented night market. Saigon connecting it all — Notre Dame, the Post Office, and the Café Apartment Block on Nguyen Hue. The Dragon Fruit Farm on the drive between Mui Ne and Da Lat completes the route.
hero.heroImageAlt: Da Lat highland flower city Truc Lam cable car Vietnam

Truc Lam Pagoda one-way cable car
All meals as specified (B, L, D)

overview.heading: 5 Days Best of Southern Vietnam + Da Lat Flower City
body: This five-day package is the most diverse South Vietnam itinerary available — connecting three fundamentally different landscapes: the Mui Ne beach and dune coast, the Da Lat highland plateau, and Ho Chi Minh City. Each destination is a different altitude, a different climate, and a different version of Vietnam. It is the only South Vietnam itinerary that covers all three regions in a single continuous journey.

Da Lat receives two nights — the right amount for a city that rewards wandering. The Crazy House (Hang Nga Guesthouse), the Linh Phuoc Dragon Temple (Vietnam's most elaborate mosaic pagoda), and the 7-Tiered Linh An Pagoda on Pren Pass are on day three. Truc Lam Pagoda — accessible by cable car over Tuyen Lam Lake — is a working Zen Buddhist monastery built in 1993, the only major pagoda in Da Lat accessible by cable car, with the lake valley and Da Lat's pine-covered ridges visible from the gondola. The one-way cable car (descent) is included.

Between Mui Ne and Da Lat on day two, the route passes through dragon fruit country — Binh Thuan Province produces 60% of Vietnam's dragon fruit export, and the Dragon Fruit Farm stop allows a farm visit where the bright pink-skinned fruit grows in rows of tall cactus, tended by the farm family who have been growing it since the 1990s. The fruit is harvested and tasted fresh at the farm.

highlights.heading: Why This 5-Day Southern Vietnam + Da Lat Package Stands Apart

items.title: Truc Lam Pagoda — Cable Car over Tuyen Lam Lake
items.body: Truc Lam Pagoda (Bamboo Forest Monastery) is a working Zen Buddhist monastery built in 1993 on a forested hillside above Tuyen Lam Lake, 5 km south of Da Lat. The cable car from Robin Hill descends over the lake — its reflection shifting between silver and blue — to the monastery entrance. The complex includes meditation halls, a lotus pond, bonsai garden, and pine-shaded walking paths. The one-way cable car (descent to the monastery, the shorter direction) is included. The lake below — a dam lake surrounded by pine forest — is the largest in the Central Highlands.
items.imageAlt: Truc Lam Pagoda cable car Tuyen Lam Lake Da Lat Vietnam

items.title: Linh Phuoc Pagoda — Vietnam's Dragon Temple
items.body: Linh Phuoc Pagoda at Trai Mat village, 8 km from Da Lat centre, was built between 1994 and 2000. The main hall is covered in an elaborate dragon mosaic composed of 12,000 beer bottles and ceramic shards — a continuous 49-metre dragon in emerald, gold, and blue that winds around the exterior. The 9-storey bell tower (49m) is the tallest in Vietnam. Inside: an 11.5-metre standing Buddha in white porcelain. The level of detail in the mosaic — done entirely by hand over six years — makes Linh Phuoc the most visually dense temple in the country.
items.imageAlt: Linh Phuoc Pagoda dragon temple mosaic Da Lat Vietnam

items.title: Mui Ne Fishing Village & Sand Dunes
items.body: Day one in Mui Ne covers the coastal highlights in sequence: the working fishing village (bamboo coracle boats, junk fleet, morning wet market), the Fairy Stream (red clay canyon walk, ankle-deep through clear water), the sand dunes (Red Sand Dunes 2 km from town, best at sunset; White Sand Dunes 25 km north, best at dawn). The ATV ride through the White Dunes is optional at own expense on this 5-day itinerary (for the included ATV version, see the 4-day Saigon Mui Ne package).
items.imageAlt: Mui Ne fishing village sand dunes Fairy Stream Vietnam

items.title: Dragon Fruit Farm — En Route Binh Thuan
items.body: The drive from Mui Ne to Da Lat passes through the Dragon Fruit producing heartland of Binh Thuan Province — where over 60% of Vietnam's dragon fruit is grown. A farm stop en route allows a guided walk through the growing rows (tall cactus trained on concrete posts, the vivid pink fruit hanging from the spiny pads), fresh fruit tasting, and a conversation with the farming family. Dragon fruit in Vietnam comes in three varieties: white flesh (standard), red flesh (sweetest), and yellow skin (the rarest and most expensive). The tasting at source is distinctly different from supermarket dragon fruit.
items.imageAlt: Dragon fruit farm Binh Thuan Vietnam Mui Ne Da Lat route

itinerary.heading: Your 5-Day Best of Southern Vietnam Itinerary

days.title: Arrive Saigon — Transfer to Mui Ne
days.description: Arrive at Tan Son Nhat International Airport (Ho Chi Minh City). Transfer to Mui Ne (225 km, 3.5 hours). Hotel check-in and dinner at beach restaurant. Afternoon/evening: Mui Ne fishing village walk, Red Sand Dunes (2 km from resort town — best at sunset, orange dunes against the South China Sea). Free evening.

days.title: Mui Ne Morning — Dragon Fruit Farm — Da Lat
days.description: Morning: Fairy Stream walk (red clay canyon, bare feet through ankle-deep clear water, 30 minutes each way). Sand dunes visit — White Sand Dunes or Red Sand Dunes, ATV optional at own expense. Lunch at Mui Ne beach restaurant. Afternoon drive to Da Lat (approximately 3.5 hours via National Road 28, climbing from sea level to 1,500 metres through Binh Thuan and Lam Dong provinces). Stop at Dragon Fruit Farm en route (Binh Thuan Province) — guided walk through growing rows, fresh tasting. Arrive Da Lat evening. Da Lat Night Market dinner.
suggestedActivities: Fairy Stream red clay canyon walk
Checkout and drive to Da Lat (3.5 hrs)
Dragon Fruit Farm stop en route

days.title: Da Lat — Crazy House, Linh Phuoc, Truc Lam
days.description: Morning: Crazy House (Hang Nga Guesthouse — no straight walls, animal-themed rooms, rooftop Da Lat views). Summer Palace of the Last Emperor (Bao Dai's Villa, 1930s French-influenced former royal hunting lodge, now open as a museum). 7-Tiered Linh Phuoc Dragon Pagoda (12,000 beer-bottle mosaic dragon exterior, 49m bell tower, 11.5m Buddha). Lunch. Afternoon: Van Hanh Zen Monastery (contemporary Buddhist complex with giant golden Buddha visible from the road). Truc Lam Pagoda — one-way cable car descent over Tuyen Lam Lake (included) to the working Zen monastery (lotus ponds, meditation halls, bonsai garden). Tuyen Lam Lake — boat or kayak at own expense. Da Lat Night Market dinner.
suggestedActivities: Summer Palace of Last Emperor (Bao Dai's Villa)
Truc Lam Pagoda cable car (1-way, included)

days.title: Da Lat to Saigon — City Tour
days.description: Drive from Da Lat to Ho Chi Minh City (5 hours, descending from 1,500m through pine forest and coffee plantations to the Saigon lowlands). Arrive HCMC for city tour: Notre Dame Cathedral (1880 French Gothic), General Post Office (1891 colonial building, still operating). Lunch. Hotel check-in. Afternoon: Nguyen Hue pedestrian boulevard, 42 Nguyen Hue Café Apartment Block, Ben Thanh Market. Dinner at local restaurant.
suggestedActivities: Drive Da Lat to Saigon (5 hrs)

days.title: Saigon Free Morning — Depart
days.description: Free morning in Ho Chi Minh City. Options: War Remnants Museum, Independence Palace, Cho Lon Chinatown, or a final Nguyen Hue café. Transfer to Tan Son Nhat International Airport per agreed schedule.

whyBook.heading: Southern Vietnam's Three Landscapes — By Malaysia's Trusted Agency Since 1988
whyBook.body: The Mui Ne–Da Lat–Saigon circuit is one of the most varied itineraries in South Vietnam — three different climates, three different landscapes, and three very different Vietnam experiences. Dhesu has been arranging South Vietnam circuits for Malaysian travellers since 1988. The Dragon Fruit Farm, the Truc Lam cable car timing, and the Da Lat Night Market — our guides know the details that make the difference.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. South Vietnam highland-coast circuits are among our most distinctive offerings for Malaysian travellers.

points.title: 35+ Years of Vietnam Experience
points.body: Since 1988, we have been arranging Vietnam journeys for Malaysians. Our guides in Mui Ne, Da Lat, and Saigon are trusted long-term partners.

points.title: Truc Lam Cable Car Included
points.body: The Truc Lam Pagoda one-way cable car (descent over Tuyen Lam Lake to the working Zen monastery) is included in the package. No separate ticketing needed.

points.title: Three South Vietnam Landscapes in Five Days
points.body: Beach coast, highland flower city, and colonial metropolis — this package covers all three Southern Vietnam landscapes that most tours visit separately.

sections.heading: 5 Day Southern Vietnam Da Lat Flower City Tour from Malaysia
sections.body:
The 5-day Best of Southern Vietnam + Da Lat package is the most varied South Vietnam itinerary for Malaysian travellers. It connects three fundamentally different destinations — the Mui Ne sand dune coast, the Da Lat highland plateau at 1,500 metres, and Ho Chi Minh City — in a single continuous journey with all logistics arranged.

Da Lat stands out as the most surprising destination in South Vietnam for Malaysian visitors: a temperate highland city (15–25°C year-round) with pine forests, French colonial architecture, strawberry farms, and the most unusual architectural attraction in Vietnam — the Crazy House (Hang Nga Guesthouse). The Truc Lam Pagoda cable car and the Linh Phuoc Dragon Temple add spiritual and visual dimension that the coast and city cannot replicate.

faq.heading: Questions About the 5-Day Southern Vietnam + Da Lat Package

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for Vietnam?
items.answer: No. Malaysian passport holders enter Vietnam visa-free for up to 30 days. Arrive at Tan Son Nhat International Airport with your Malaysian passport.

items.question: Is the Truc Lam Pagoda cable car return or one-way?
items.answer: The package includes the one-way cable car descent from Robin Hill to Truc Lam Pagoda (the direction that allows you to arrive at the monastery entrance from above, with the lake view on the descent). The return from the monastery to the road can be done by the cable car (at own expense for the return direction) or by walking back up the monastery hill on foot (15 minutes). Most visitors take the cable car one-way and return by the monastery path.

items.question: What is the Dragon Fruit Farm experience?
items.answer: The Dragon Fruit Farm is a working family-owned plantation in Binh Thuan Province on the road between Mui Ne and Da Lat. A guided walk through the growing rows (tall cactus pads trained on concrete posts with the bright pink fruit hanging from them) is followed by a fresh tasting of multiple varieties — white flesh, red flesh, and sometimes yellow-skin varieties. The farm also sells dried dragon fruit and local honey. The stop takes approximately 30–45 minutes.

items.question: What are the optional extras in Da Lat?
items.answer: Several Da Lat activities are at own expense: the Tuyen Lam Lake boat or kayak (approximately RM15–25 per person), the Clay Tunnels / clay sculpture garden near the lake, the lavender garden, and the return direction of the Truc Lam cable car. The ATV ride through Mui Ne's White Sand Dunes is also at own expense on this package (it is included in the 4-day Saigon Mui Ne package).
